_comment: Project-level Claude Code settings shipped with the template. Four hooks enforce session-durability habits: (1) Stop blocks if HANDOFF.md is stale, forcing a resume-snapshot every session; (2) Stop archives the raw session jsonl into .session-archive/ so full-fidelity history survives; (3) PreCompact mirrors the archive and asks Claude to refresh HANDOFF.md before auto-compaction summarizes the conversation away; (4) PostCompact re-injects HANDOFF.md (and any other critical state your project adds) as additionalContext after compaction, so the freshly compacted model immediately knows what was being worked on. Delete any hook you don't want, but at least keep the HANDOFF Stop hook — it is the single most load-bearing habit in this template.

hooks.command: HANDOFF="${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR:-$PWD}/HANDOFF.md"; if [ -f "$HANDOFF" ]; then mtime=$(stat -c %Y "$HANDOFF" 2>/dev/null || stat -f %m "$HANDOFF" 2>/dev/null); age=$(( $(date +%s) - mtime )); [ "$age" -lt 1800 ] && exit 0; fi; printf '%s\n' "{\"decision\":\"block\",\"reason\":\"Before ending this session, write or update HANDOFF.md at $HANDOFF so work can be resumed if the session is killed. Include these sections: ## Current Task, ## Files Touched (path + role), ## Done (completed this session), ## In Progress (active edits, partial thoughts, hypotheses to test), ## Next Step (the exact next action). Overwrite the whole file each time — it is a snapshot, not a log. After writing, stop normally; the hook will see the fresh mtime and let you stop.\"}"

INPUT=$(cat); SID=$(echo "$INPUT" | jq -r .session_id 2>/dev/null); PROJ_DIR="${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR:-$PWD}"; SLUG=$(echo "$PROJ_DIR" | tr / -); SRC="$HOME/.claude/projects/$SLUG/$SID.jsonl"; DST_DIR="$PROJ_DIR/.session-archive"; mkdir -p "$DST_DIR"; [ -f "$SRC" ] && cp "$SRC" "$DST_DIR/$SID.jsonl" 2>/dev/null; exit 0

INPUT=$(cat); SID=$(echo "$INPUT" | jq -r .session_id 2>/dev/null); PROJ_DIR="${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR:-$PWD}"; SLUG=$(echo "$PROJ_DIR" | tr / -); SRC="$HOME/.claude/projects/$SLUG/$SID.jsonl"; DST_DIR="$PROJ_DIR/.session-archive"; mkdir -p "$DST_DIR"; [ -f "$SRC" ] && cp "$SRC" "$DST_DIR/$(date +%Y%m%d-%H%M%S)-precompact-$SID.jsonl" 2>/dev/null; exit 0

PROJ="${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR:-/workspace}"; SLUG=$(echo "$PROJ" | tr / -); SESS_DIR="$HOME/.claude/projects/$SLUG"; LATEST=$(ls -t "$SESS_DIR"/*.jsonl 2>/dev/null | head -1); [ -z "$LATEST" ] && exit 0; timeout 240 claude -p --permission-mode bypassPermissions --allowedTools 'Read Write Bash' --model sonnet --no-session-persistence "You are a PreCompact hook. Refresh $PROJ/HANDOFF.md before this conversation gets compacted, so no critical state is lost in summarization. Read the session transcript at $LATEST (large file; use Read with offset/limit if needed). Overwrite (do not append) $PROJ/HANDOFF.md with these sections: ## Current Task (one paragraph: what is actively being worked on and why). ## Files Touched (bullet list of absolute path + role + version/commit if applicable). ## Done (commits pushed this session: short SHA + one-line why; include verified outcomes like 'v2.77 stable for 4 h'). ## In Progress (hypotheses under test, specific file paths + line numbers, any secrets shared by name + storage location only NEVER the value, scheduled wakeups with their trigger times). ## Next Step (single exact next action; if waiting on the user, state what we're waiting for). Be terse but specific. A reader who hasn't seen the conversation must be able to resume work. After writing, exit." >/dev/null 2>&1 || true; exit 0
